title: Separate liveness and readiness probes for DB health
summary: Minimal, focused change that replaces the Keystone API liveness probe from HTTPGet /v3 to TCPSocket on port 5000, separating liveness from readiness. The readiness probe (HTTPGet /v3) remains unchanged. Implementation exactly matches the plan with no scope creep. Unit tests assert both the presence of TCPSocket and the absence of HTTPGet (regression guard). Documentation updated with a Type column and rationale paragraph. All automated checks pass: go vet clean, golangci-lint clean (one pre-existing warning in unrelated file), all 47 unit tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: Liveness probe asserts TCPSocket not nil
Liveness probe asserts HTTPGet is nil (regression guard)

code_quality_checklist.item: CC-0062 feature ID referenced in comments

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
Follows Kubernetes probe best practices (liveness=process, readiness=function)
No scope creep into Helm chart (operator probes are different from API probes)

defensive_checklist.item: No external inputs to validate in this change
